content.claim: Hungary historically responded to rural enrollment decline by reducing parallel classes within schools rather than closing/merging schools, thereby shifting segregation dynamics to between-school rather than within-school level, unlike Finland's consolidation trajectory.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Comparison is structural/institutional rather than outcome-based; no data on whether within-school or between-school segregation produced worse achievement disparities
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
